seoTitle: How to Edit the Freshdesk Dashboard
metaDescription: Learn how to customize your Freshdesk dashboard so it shows the ticket metrics and widgets that matter most to you.
intro: Your Freshdesk dashboard is the first thing you see when you log in, and customizing it helps you focus on the metrics that matter to your role. This guide shows how to edit it.

steps.heading: Open your dashboard
steps.body: Log in to Freshdesk and go to the main dashboard view.

steps.heading: Enter edit mode
steps.body: Find the option to customize or edit the dashboard layout, usually available as a button or icon on the page.

steps.heading: Add, remove, or rearrange widgets
steps.body: Choose which widgets to display, such as ticket volume, unresolved tickets, or agent performance, and arrange them in your preferred order.

steps.heading: Save your layout
steps.body: Save the changes so your customized dashboard loads automatically the next time you log in.

faq.question: Can every agent customize their own dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, dashboard customization is typically personal to each agent's view.

faq.question: What kinds of widgets can I add?
faq.answer: Common widgets include ticket counts by status, response time metrics, and agent performance summaries.

faq.question: Can I reset the dashboard to default?
faq.answer: Most dashboards offer a way to reset back to the default layout if you want to start over.

faq.question: Does editing the dashboard change actual ticket data?
faq.answer: No, dashboard customization only changes how information is displayed, not the underlying ticket data.
